Vinod Sapkal Assistant Professor Department of Information Technology Vasantdada Patil Pratishthan's College of Engineering & Visual Arts UNIVERSITY OF MUMBAI (AY 2025-26) CERTIFICATE This is to certify that the project entitled “Fake News Detector Using Machine Learning” is bonafide work of Anand Dane vu4f2223094, Swaroop Sawant vu4f2223100, Omkar Patil vu4f2223108, Prathamesh Thakare vu4f2223103, submitted to University of Mumbai in partial fulfillment of requirement for the award of degree of “Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology-Semester-VII” Dr.
This project focuses on developing a Fake News Detection System using Machine Learning techniques.
Therefore, developing an automated system using Machine Learning to detect fake or misleading information has become essential.
PROPOSED SYSTEM The proposed Fake News Detection System uses supervised machine learning methods to classify news articles as real or fake.
By combining both fake and real articles, this dataset allows us to train a machine learning model to detect patterns in the text that can help identify misinformation.

TF-IDF Vectorizer (Feature Extraction): Although not a learning algorithm by itself, the TF-IDF (Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency) vectorizer plays a crucial role in converting text data into numerical form so that machine learning models can work with it.
Conclusion: Thus, we have successfully built and implemented a Fake News Detection system using machine learning techniques.
